decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat distribution away from the chip center. I will increase the spreader conductivity by a factor of 1.5 (from 425.25 to ~637.88), which is within the allowed 2.0x step limit and below the 500.0 bound is violated so I must cap at 500.0 or adjust ratio. Wait, bounds are [20.0, 500.0]. Current is 425.25. Max allowed is 500.0. So I cannot do 1.5x. I will set it to the maximum bound of 500.0 to maximize heat spreading capability.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity to its upper bound maximizes lateral heat spreading, directly reducing the localized peak temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches physical limits of the material model
If this single change is insufficient, geometric changes (spreader width/height) will be required in the next iteration
